title: Noronhia mannii
taxonomy: Noronhia mannii is a species within the family Oleaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants. However, some sources may group it under the family Oliniaceae, which is a smaller family often considered a subfamily of Oleaceae. The genus Noronhia is typically classified as a part of the family Oliniaceae. This species is native to Madagascar and is commonly known for its unique and distinctive characteristics. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of Noronhia mannii within the plant kingdom.
uses: Noronhia mannii is reported to have various medicinal properties. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
